Assert Section Signals Invalid JSON For Array With String Values
### I have checked the following:

- [x] I have searched existing issues and found nothing related to my issue.

### This bug is:

- [ ] making Bruno unusable for me
- [ ] slowing me down but I'm able to continue working
- [x] annoying
- [x] this feature was working in a previous version but is broken in the current release.

### Bruno version

3.1.4

### Operating System

macOS Tahoe 26.3.1 (a)

### Describe the bug

I usually have some basic assertions for all requests. I noticed that Bruno is failing a simple `res.body` `isJson` assertion now for some responses that are just a collection of string values; for instance:

```javascript
[
  "A55001213ZX0A"
]
```

### .bru file to reproduce the bug

```bru
meta {
  name: Some Identifier
  type: http
}

get {
  url: {{api-host}}/resource/{{resource-id}}
  body: none
}

headers {
  Accept: application/json
}

vars:pre-request {
  resource-id: A55001213ZX0A
}

assert {
  res.status: eq 200
  res.body: isJson
}
```

### Screenshots/Live demo link

N/A

    12	chai.use(function (chai, utils) {
    13	  // Custom assertion for checking if a variable is JSON
    14	  chai.Assertion.addProperty('json', function () {
    15	    const obj = this._obj;
    16	    // Use Object.prototype.toString instead of constructor check for cross-realm compatibility.
    17	    // Objects created inside Node's vm.createContext() have a different Object constructor,
    18	    // so obj.constructor === Object fails for objects passed via res.setBody() from scripts.
    19	    // Note: toString check is more permissive than constructor check ŌĆö custom class instances
    20	    const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && !Array.isArray(obj)
    21	      && Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]';
    22	
    23	    this.assert(isJson, `expected ${utils.inspect(obj)} to be JSON`, `expected ${utils.inspect(obj)} not to be JSON`);
    24	  });
    25	});

    30	    const obj = this._obj;
    31	    let match = false;
    32	    if (obj === undefined) {
    33	      match = false;
    34	    } else {

    78	const parseAssertionOperator = (str = '') => {
    79	  if (!str || typeof str !== 'string' || !str.length) {
    80	    return {
    81	      operator: 'eq',
    82	      value: str
    83	    };

   132	  const [operator, ...rest] = str.trim().split(' ');
   133	  const value = rest.join(' ');
   134	
   135	  if (unaryOperators.includes(operator)) {
   136	    return {
   137	      operator,
   138	      value: ''
   139	    };
   140	  }
   141	
   142	  if (operators.includes(operator)) {
   143	    return {
   144	      operator,
   145	      value
   146	    };
   147	  }
   148	
   149	  return {
   150	    operator: 'eq',

   152	  };

   154	
   155	const isUnaryOperator = (operator) => {
   156	  const unaryOperators = [
   157	    'isEmpty',
   158	    'isNotEmpty',
   159	    'isNull',
   160	    'isUndefined',
   161	    'isDefined',
   162	    'isTruthy',

   167	    'isBoolean',
   168	    'isArray'
   169	  ];
   170	
   171	  return unaryOperators.includes(operator);

   174	const evaluateJsTemplateLiteralBasedOnRuntime = (literal, context, runtime) => {
   175	  if (runtime === 'quickjs') {
   176	    return executeQuickJsVm({
   177	      script: literal,
   178	      context,
   179	      scriptType: 'template-literal'
   180	    });
   181	  }
   182	
   183	  return evaluateJsTemplateLiteral(literal, context);
   184	};
   185	
   186	const evaluateJsExpressionBasedOnRuntime = (expr, context, runtime) => {
   187	  if (runtime === 'quickjs') {
   188	    return executeQuickJsVm({
   189	      script: expr,
   190	      context,
   191	      scriptType: 'expression'
   192	    });
   193	  }
   194	
   195	  return evaluateJsExpression(expr, context);
   196	};
   197	
   198	const evaluateRhsOperand = (rhsOperand, operator, context, runtime) => {
   199	  if (isUnaryOperator(operator)) {
   200	    return;
   201	  }
   202	
   203	  const interpolationContext = {
   204	    globalEnvironmentVariables: context.bru.globalEnvironmentVariables,
   205	    collectionVariables: context.bru.collectionVariables,
   206	    folderVariables: context.bru.folderVariables,
   207	    requestVariables: context.bru.requestVariables,
   208	    runtimeVariables: context.bru.runtimeVariables,
   209	    envVariables: context.bru.envVariables,
   210	    processEnvVars: context.bru.processEnvVars
   211	  };
   212	
   213	  // gracefully allow both a,b as well as [a, b]
   214	  if (operator === 'in' || operator === 'notIn') {
   215	    if (rhsOperand.startsWith('[') && rhsOperand.endsWith(']')) {
   216	      rhsOperand = rhsOperand.substring(1, rhsOperand.length - 1);
   217	    }
   218	
   219	    return rhsOperand
   220	      .split(',')
   221	      .map((v) =>
   222	        evaluateJsTemplateLiteralBasedOnRuntime(interpolateString(v.trim(), interpolationContext), context, runtime)
   223	      );
   224	  }
   225	
   226	  if (operator === 'between') {
   227	    const [lhs, rhs] = rhsOperand
   228	      .split(',')
   229	      .map((v) =>
   230	        evaluateJsTemplateLiteralBasedOnRuntime(interpolateString(v.trim(), interpolationContext), context, runtime)
   231	      );
   232	    return [lhs, rhs];
   233	  }
   234	
   235	  // gracefully allow both ^[a-Z] as well as /^[a-Z]/
   236	  if (operator === 'matches' || operator === 'notMatches') {
   237	    if (rhsOperand.startsWith('/') && rhsOperand.endsWith('/')) {
   238	      rhsOperand = rhsOperand.substring(1, rhsOperand.length - 1);
   239	    }
   240	
   241	    return interpolateString(rhsOperand, interpolationContext);
   242	  }
   243	
   244	  return evaluateJsTemplateLiteralBasedOnRuntime(interpolateString(rhsOperand, interpolationContext), context, runtime);
   245	};
   246	
   247	class AssertRuntime {
   248	  constructor(props) {
   249	    this.runtime = props?.runtime || 'quickjs';
   250	  }
   251	
   252	  runAssertions(assertions, request, response, envVariables, runtimeVariables, processEnvVars) {
   253	    const globalEnvironmentVariables = request?.globalEnvironmentVariables || {};
   254	    const oauth2CredentialVariables = request?.oauth2CredentialVariables || {};
   255	    const collectionVariables = request?.collectionVariables || {};
   256	    const folderVariables = request?.folderVariables || {};
   257	    const requestVariables = request?.requestVariables || {};
   258	    const enabledAssertions = _.filter(assertions, (a) => a.enabled);
   259	    if (!enabledAssertions.length) {
   260	      return [];
   261	    }
   262	
   263	    const promptVariables = request?.promptVariables || {};
   264	    const certsAndProxyConfig = request?.certsAndProxyConfig;
   265	    const bru = new Bru({
   266	      runtime: this.runtime,
   267	      envVariables,
   268	      runtimeVariables,
   269	      processEnvVars,
   270	      collectionVariables,
   271	      folderVariables,
   272	      requestVariables,
   273	      globalEnvironmentVariables,
   274	      promptVariables,
   275	      certsAndProxyConfig,
   276	      requestUrl: request?.url
   277	    });
   278	    const req = new BrunoRequest(request);
   279	    const res = createResponseParser(response);
   280	
   281	    const bruContext = {
   282	      bru,
   283	      req,
   284	      res
   285	    };
   286	
   287	    const context = {
   288	      ...globalEnvironmentVariables,
   289	      ...collectionVariables,
   290	      ...envVariables,
   291	      ...folderVariables,
   292	      ...requestVariables,
   293	      ...oauth2CredentialVariables,
   294	      ...runtimeVariables,
   295	      ...processEnvVars,
   296	      ...bruContext
   297	    };
   298	
   299	    const assertionResults = [];
   300	
   301	    // parse assertion operators
   302	    for (const v of enabledAssertions) {
   303	      const lhsExpr = v.name;
   304	      const rhsExpr = v.value;
   305	      const { operator, value: rhsOperand } = parseAssertionOperator(rhsExpr);
   306	
   307	      try {
   308	        const lhs = evaluateJsExpressionBasedOnRuntime(lhsExpr, context, this.runtime);
   309	        const rhs = evaluateRhsOperand(rhsOperand, operator, context, this.runtime);
   310	
   311	        switch (operator) {
   312	          case 'eq':
   313	            expect(lhs).to.equal(rhs);
   314	            break;
   315	          case 'neq':
   316	            expect(lhs).to.not.equal(rhs);
   317	            break;
   318	          case 'gt':
   319	            expect(lhs).to.be.greaterThan(rhs);
   320	            break;
   321	          case 'gte':
   322	            expect(lhs).to.be.greaterThanOrEqual(rhs);
   323	            break;
   324	          case 'lt':
   325	            expect(lhs).to.be.lessThan(rhs);
   326	            break;
   327	          case 'lte':
   328	            expect(lhs).to.be.lessThanOrEqual(rhs);
   329	            break;
   330	          case 'in':
   331	            expect(lhs).to.be.oneOf(rhs);
   332	            break;
   333	          case 'notIn':
   334	            expect(lhs).to.not.be.oneOf(rhs);
   335	            break;
   336	          case 'contains':
   337	            expect(lhs).to.include(rhs);
   338	            break;
   339	          case 'notContains':
   340	            expect(lhs).to.not.include(rhs);
   341	            break;
   342	          case 'length':
   343	            expect(lhs).to.have.lengthOf(rhs);
   344	            break;
   345	          case 'matches':
   346	            expect(lhs).to.match(new RegExp(rhs));
   347	            break;
   348	          case 'notMatches':
   349	            expect(lhs).to.not.match(new RegExp(rhs));
   350	            break;
   351	          case 'startsWith':
   352	            expect(lhs).to.startWith(rhs);
   353	            break;
   354	          case 'endsWith':
   355	            expect(lhs).to.endWith(rhs);
   356	            break;
   357	          case 'between':
   358	            const [min, max] = rhs;
   359	            expect(lhs).to.be.within(min, max);
   360	            break;
   361	          case 'isEmpty':
   362	            expect(lhs).to.be.empty;
   363	            break;
   364	          case 'isNotEmpty':
   365	            expect(lhs).to.not.be.empty;
   366	            break;
   367	          case 'isNull':
   368	            expect(lhs).to.be.null;
   369	            break;
   370	          case 'isUndefined':
   371	            expect(lhs).to.be.undefined;
   372	            break;
   373	          case 'isDefined':
   374	            expect(lhs).to.not.be.undefined;
   375	            break;
   376	          case 'isTruthy':
   377	            expect(lhs).to.be.true;
   378	            break;
   379	          case 'isFalsy':
   380	            expect(lhs).to.be.false;
   381	            break;
   382	          case 'isJson':
   383	            expect(lhs).to.be.json;
   384	            break;
   385	          case 'isNumber':
   386	            expect(lhs).to.be.a('number');
   387	            break;
   388	          case 'isString':
   389	            expect(lhs).to.be.a('string');
   390	            break;
   391	          case 'isBoolean':
   392	            expect(lhs).to.be.a('boolean');
   393	            break;
   394	          case 'isArray':
   395	            expect(lhs).to.be.a('array');
   396	            break;
   397	          default:
   398	            expect(lhs).to.equal(rhs);
   399	            break;
   400	        }
   401	
   402	        assertionResults.push({
   403	          uid: nanoid(),
   404	          lhsExpr,
   405	          rhsExpr,
   406	          rhsOperand,
   407	          operator,
   408	          status: 'pass'
   409	        });
   410	      } catch (err) {
   411	        assertionResults.push({
   412	          uid: nanoid(),
   413	          lhsExpr,
   414	          rhsExpr,
   415	          rhsOperand,
   416	          operator,
   417	          status: 'fail',
   418	          error: err.message
   419	        });
   420	      }
   421	    }
   422	
   423	    request.assertionResults = assertionResults;
   424	
   425	    return assertionResults;
   426	  }
   427	}
   428	
   429	module.exports = AssertRuntime;
   430

Now I can see the issue. Let's look at both locations where `isJson` validation happens:

1. In `packages/bruno-js/src/runtime/assert-runtime.js` (lines 14-24) - the custom chai assertion
2. In `packages/bruno-js/src/sandbox/quickjs/shims/test.js` - the QuickJS shim version

Both have the same issue. The check on line 20-21:

```javascript
const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && !Array.isArray(obj)
  && Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]';
```

This check rejects arrays! But an array like `["A55001213ZX0A"]` is valid JSON. The issue is that `isJson` asserts that the value must be a JSON **object** (not an array), but JSON can be an array too.

2026-05-30 07:56:12,806 - INFO - swea-agent-usebruno__bruno-7620 - ­¤ÆŁ THOUGHT
Now I can see both locations. The issue is clear:

**The current `isJson` check:**
```javascript
const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && !Array.isArray(obj)
  && Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]';
```

This says: "isJson = is an object AND is NOT an array AND is specifically a plain Object". This rejects arrays and other valid JSON types.

**What `isJson` should do:** Verify that the value is valid JSON. What does "valid JSON" mean? In the context of `res.body`, `isJson` should check if the body is valid JSON - which means it should be a JSON object OR a JSON array. When Bruno parses a response body as JSON, the result is either an object or an array (since JSON.parse can return either).

Let me check the test file to understand the expected behavior better

Interesting! Looking at the test file, the test on line 320-326 explicitly says:

```javascript
it('should fail for an array', () => {
  const results = runAssertions(
    [{ name: 'res.body', value: 'isJson', enabled: true }],
    makeResponse([1, 2, 3])
  );
  expect(results[0].status).toBe('fail');
});
```

So according to the current tests, `isJson` is **supposed** to fail for arrays. But the PR description says it's a bug that arrays with string values fail `isJson`.

Wait, let me re-read the PR description. The user says:
> "I noticed that Bruno is failing a simple `res.body` `isJson` assertion now for some responses that are just a collection of string values"

So the user expects `isJson` to pass for a JSON array. The current behavior (as shown in tests) is that it fails for arrays. But the PR description says this feature was working in a previous version but is broken now.

Let me check if there's perhaps another interpretation. Maybe `isJson` is supposed to check that the response body is valid JSON (as a string), not that the parsed body is a JSON object. Let me look at how `res.body` works:

   106	const parseDataFromResponse = (response, disableParsingResponseJson = false) => {
   107	  // Parse the charset from content type: https://stackoverflow.com/a/33192813
   108	  const charsetMatch = /charset=([^()<>@,;:"/[\]?.=\s]*)/i.exec(response.headers['content-type'] || '');
   109	  // https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/RegExp/exec#using_exec_with_regexp_literals
   110	  const charsetValue = charsetMatch?.[1];
   111	  const dataBuffer = Buffer.from(response.data);
   112	  // Overwrite the original data for backwards compatibility
   113	  let data;
   114	  if (iconv.encodingExists(charsetValue)) {
   115	    data = iconv.decode(dataBuffer, charsetValue);
   116	  } else {
   117	    data = iconv.decode(dataBuffer, 'utf-8');
   118	  }
   119	  // Try to parse response to JSON, this can quietly fail
   120	  try {
   121	    // Filter out ZWNBSP character
   122	    // https://gist.github.com/antic183/619f42b559b78028d1fe9e7ae8a1352d
   123	    data = data.replace(/^\uFEFF/, '');
   124	    if (!disableParsingResponseJson) {
   125	      data = JSON.parse(data);
   126	    }
   127	  } catch { }
   128	
   129	  return { data, dataBuffer };
   130	};
   131	
   132	const parseDataFromRequest = (request) => {
   133	  let requestDataString;
   134	
   135	  // File uploads are redacted, multipart FormData is formatted from original data for readability, and other types are stringified as-is.
   136	  if (request.mode === 'file') {
   137	    requestDataString = '<request body redacted>';
   138	  } else if (isFormData(request?.data) && Array.isArray(request._originalMultipartData)) {
   139	    const boundary = request.data._boundary || 'boundary';
   140	    requestDataString = formatMultipartData(request._originalMultipartData, boundary);
   141	  } else {
   142	    requestDataString = typeof request?.data === 'string' ? request?.data : safeStringifyJSON(request?.data);
   143	  }
   144	
   145	  const requestCopy = cloneDeep(request);
   146	  if (!requestCopy.data) {
   147	    return { data: null, dataBuffer: null };
   148	  }
   149	  requestCopy.data = requestDataString;
   150	  return parseDataFromResponse(requestCopy);
   151	};
   152	
   153	module.exports = {
   154	  uuid,
   155	  stringifyJson,
   156	  parseJson,
   157	  safeStringifyJSON,
   158	  safeParseJSON,
   159	  simpleHash,
   160	  generateUidBasedOnHash,
   161	  flattenDataForDotNotation,
   162	  parseDataFromResponse,
   163	  parseDataFromRequest
   164	};

Now I understand the full picture. When a response comes back with a JSON content type, Bruno parses the body with `JSON.parse()`. The result can be either an object (`{}`) or an array (`[]`). 

When the user sets `res.body: isJson`, they want to verify that the response body is valid JSON. Since the body has already been parsed by `JSON.parse`, `res.body` will be either an object, array, string, number, boolean, or null.

The current `isJson` check rejects arrays. But the user expects arrays to pass too, since `["A55001213ZX0A"]` is valid JSON.

However, looking at the test file (line 320-326), there's a test that explicitly expects arrays to FAIL. This seems to be the existing (incorrect) behavior that the PR wants to fix.

Now I have a clear understanding of the issue. The bug is in two files:

1. `packages/bruno-js/src/runtime/assert-runtime.js` (lines 20-21)

But wait, let me think about what "isJson" should mean. Looking at the operator name, it says "is json". JSON can be:
- An object `{}`
- An array `[]`
- A string `"hello"`
- A number `42`
- A boolean `true/false`
- null

But in Bruno's context, `res.body` is the parsed response body. If the body is valid JSON, it gets parsed. If it's not valid JSON, it stays as a string. So `isJson` should check: "Is this a parsed JSON value?" - i.e., is it NOT a string (assuming non-JSON responses remain as strings)?

But the current implementation checks for `typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && !Array.isArray(obj)`. This is too restrictive - it only allows plain objects.

> "I noticed that Bruno is failing a simple `res.body` `isJson` assertion now for some responses that are just a collection of string values; for instance: `["A55001213ZX0A"]`"

The user expects that `["A55001213ZX0A"]` should pass `isJson`. The most logical fix is to also allow arrays. But should strings, numbers, booleans, and null also pass? Those are also valid JSON.

Given the context (checking if a response body is JSON), I think the intent is: the response body was successfully parsed as JSON. Since `JSON.parse` can return any JSON type, and Bruno's parser only leaves the data as a string if JSON parsing fails, the check could be simplified to: `typeof obj !== 'string'`. But this might be too broad.

```javascript

it('should pass for objects from a different realm', () => { ... expect pass });

it('should fail for a string', () => { ... expect fail });
it('should fail for null', () => { ... expect fail });
```

const chai = require('chai');
chai.use(function (chai, utils) {
  chai.Assertion.addProperty('json', function () {
    const obj = this._obj;
    const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && !Array.isArray(obj)
      && Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]';
    this.assert(isJson, 'expected ' + utils.inspect(obj) + ' to be JSON', 'expected ' + utils.inspect(obj) + ' not to be JSON');
  });
});

const { expect } = chai;

// Test 1: Object should pass
try {
  expect({ key: 'value' }).to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: Object is JSON');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL: Object is JSON -', e.message);
}

// Test 2: Array with string values should pass (but currently fails)
try {
  expect(['A55001213ZX0A']).to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: Array with strings is JSON');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL: Array with strings is JSON -', e.message);
}

// Test 3: Array with numbers should pass (but currently fails)
try {
  expect([1, 2, 3]).to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: Array with numbers is JSON');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL: Array with numbers is JSON -', e.message);
}

// Test 4: String should fail
try {
  expect('hello').to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: String is NOT JSON (correct)');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL: String is JSON -', e.message);
}

// Test 5: null should fail
try {
  expect(null).to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: null is NOT JSON (correct)');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL: null is JSON -', e.message);
}

cd /testbed && cat > /tmp/reproduce.js << 'EOF'
const chai = require('chai');

chai.use(function (chai, utils) {
  chai.Assertion.addProperty('json', function () {
    const obj = this._obj;
    const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && !Array.isArray(obj)
      && Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]';
    this.assert(isJson, 'expected ' + utils.inspect(obj) + ' to be JSON', 'expected ' + utils.inspect(obj) + ' not to be JSON');
  });
});

const { expect } = chai;

// Test 1: Object should pass
try {
  expect({ key: 'value' }).to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: Object is JSON');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL: Object is JSON -', e.message);
}

// Test 2: Array with string values should pass (but currently fails)
try {
  expect(['A55001213ZX0A']).to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: Array with strings is JSON');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL: Array with strings is JSON -', e.message);
}

// Test 3: Array with numbers should pass (but currently fails)
try {
  expect([1, 2, 3]).to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: Array with numbers is JSON');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL: Array with numbers is JSON -', e.message);
}

// Test 4: String should fail
try {
  expect('hello').to.be.json;
  console.log('FAIL: String is JSON - should have failed');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('PASS: String is NOT JSON (correct)');
}

// Test 5: null should fail
try {
  expect(null).to.be.json;
  console.log('FAIL: null is JSON - should have failed');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('PASS: null is NOT JSON (correct)');
}
EOF

const chai = require('chai');

chai.use(function (chai, utils) {
  chai.Assertion.addProperty('json', function () {
    const obj = this._obj;
    const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && !Array.isArray(obj)
      && Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]';
    this.assert(isJson, 'expected ' + utils.inspect(obj) + ' to be JSON', 'expected ' + utils.inspect(obj) + ' not to be JSON');
  });
});

const { expect } = chai;

// Test 1: Object should pass
try {
  expect({ key: 'value' }).to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: Object is JSON');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL: Object is JSON -', e.message);
}

  expect(['A55001213ZX0A']).to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: Array with strings is JSON');

  console.log('FAIL: Array with strings is JSON -', e.message);
}

  console.log('FAIL: Array with numbers is JSON -', e.message);
}

}

  console.log('FAIL: null is JSON - should have failed');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('PASS: null is NOT JSON (correct)');
}

const { expect } = chai;

// Test the custom json assertion
chai.use(function (chai, utils) {
  chai.Assertion.addProperty('json', function () {
    const obj = this._obj;
    const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && !Array.isArray(obj)
      && Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]';
    this.assert(isJson, 'expected ' + utils.inspect(obj) + ' to be JSON', 'expected ' + utils.inspect(obj) + ' not to be JSON');
  });
});

try {
  expect({ key: 'value' }).to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: Object is JSON');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL: Object is JSON -', e.message);
}

  expect(['A55001213ZX0A']).to.be.json;
  console.log('PASS: Array is JSON');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL: Array is JSON -', e.message);

```javascript
const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && !Array.isArray(obj)
  && Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]';
```

to something that allows arrays too:
```javascript
const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null 
  && (Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]' || Array.isArray(obj));
```

Or more simply:
```javascript
const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null;
```

But wait, if we just do `typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null`, that would also allow things like `new Date()`, `new RegExp()`, etc. 

Actually, let me think about this differently. The purpose of `isJson` is to check if the response body was successfully parsed as JSON. After `JSON.parse()`, the result can only be:
- an object (plain object)
- an array
- a string
- a number
- a boolean
- null

If `JSON.parse` fails, the body remains as a string. So checking `typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null` would correctly identify parsed JSON objects and arrays while excluding strings, numbers, booleans, and null.

But what about cross-realm objects? The comment says:
> "Objects created inside Node's vm.createContext() have a different Object constructor, so obj.constructor === Object fails for objects passed via res.setBody() from scripts."

So `Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]'` is used to handle cross-realm objects. For cross-realm arrays, `Array.isArray()` should still work correctly since `Array.isArray()` is realm-independent.

So the fix should be:

```javascript
const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null 
  && (Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]' || Array.isArray(obj));
```

This allows:
- Plain objects (including cross-realm ones via toString check)
- Arrays (including cross-realm ones via Array.isArray)

And rejects:
- null
- strings
- numbers
- booleans
- other object types (Date, RegExp, etc.)

Let me make the edits:</think>Now let me fix both files. First, the assert-runtime.js

      && Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '"'"'[object Object]'"'"';

      && (Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '"'"'[object Object]'"'"' || Array.isArray(obj));

// Use the actual module's implementation

// Directly test the custom chai property that AssertRuntime registers

chai.use(function (chai, utils) {
  chai.Assertion.addProperty('json', function () {
    const obj = this._obj;
    const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null
      && (Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]' || Array.isArray(obj));
    this.assert(isJson, 'expected ' + utils.inspect(obj) + ' to be JSON', 'expected ' + utils.inspect(obj) + ' not to be JSON');
  });
});

console.log('Testing FIXED isJson implementation...');

  expect({ key: 'value' }).to.be.json;
  console.log('1. PASS: Object is JSON');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('1. FAIL: Object is JSON -', e.message);
}

    12	chai.use(function (chai, utils) {
    13	  // Custom assertion for checking if a variable is JSON
    14	  chai.Assertion.addProperty('json', function () {
    15	    const obj = this._obj;
    16	    // Use Object.prototype.toString instead of constructor check for cross-realm compatibility.
    17	    // Objects created inside Node's vm.createContext() have a different Object constructor,
    18	    // so obj.constructor === Object fails for objects passed via res.setBody() from scripts.
    19	    // Note: toString check is more permissive than constructor check ŌĆö custom class instances
    20	    // Arrays are also valid JSON (e.g., ["value1", "value2"]) so we include them.
    21	    const isJson = typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null
    22	      && (Object.prototype.toString.call(obj) === '[object Object]' || Array.isArray(obj));
    23	
    24	    this.assert(isJson, `expected ${utils.inspect(obj)} to be JSON`, `expected ${utils.inspect(obj)} not to be JSON`);
    25	  });
    26	});
